what_it_does: This file is a living noticeboard guide that documents the FACE-1 breadth surfaces (channel view, board view, session drill, transcript visualizer) and a recent phone-frame responsive-form sweep, recording their shared architecture, verification status, commits, and remaining gaps.
observations: Front matter marks it as a living guide authored by projection with a reference link to board://item-78c63045.
It describes four operator-facing breadth surfaces built on one reusable clone-host pattern over DNA organisms.
The render pipeline is store -> overlay host -> DNA.faceRecord.<adapter>(raw) -> DNA.renderArchetype(<archetype>, rec, opts) -> appendChild.
Transcript-viz visualizes a 35,904-chunk corpus as a constellation: one star per session, brightness by cosine similarity, size by hit count.
A route-backend gotcha was caught before building: /api/transcript-search reads a sqlite/chroma index, not the numpy VEC_PATH the author initially assumed.
DNA's .screen class is a fixed 390px phone-frame (phone.css:98), which at 1440px pinned graph bodies to a 162px island.
Three surfaces needed one-line responsive fixes: transcript (162->412), board (commit 2ef987b), and channel (commit d8f1320).
A client-side filter was added to the 60-cap sessions roster (commit e729c98), searching title/name/cwd/state but never id.
All surfaces depend on DNA shipping the adapter plus archetype, and the FORM sweep was driven by the honest-state discipline.
Verification was performed by one operator in verify-mode; Tim has not verified on his actual device/access-path.
The channel-view's pre-session verification history is now distrusted because it had falsely logged 'verified both viewports'.
standouts: The reusable clone-host pattern: every breadth surface renders a DNA organism instead of a bespoke UI.
The phone-frame FORM sweep that exposed and fixed falsely 'verified' responsive layouts in three surfaces.
The proactive route-backend gotcha caught by curling the live /api/transcript-search before building.
The explicit distrust of prior channel-view verification and the unresolved Tim-device verification gap.
conventions: Uses YAML front matter with id, address, type, state, title, links, and history.
Uses all-caps section headers (STATUS, LANE, ABOUT, KIND, SUMMARY, ENTITIES, CLAIMS/DECISIONS, RELATIONS, OPEN_QUESTIONS/GAPS, SOURCE_ADDRESS).
Tags claims with [verified] and cites commits plus a doc source.
References other noticeboard items with board:// URIs.
Records state transitions in the history block.
concerns: channel-view had falsely logged verification, so its history is now distrusted.
Verification is only from one operator; no independent validation by Tim on a real device/access-path.
The access-path itself is noted as a real gap but not detailed here.
Heavy project-specific jargon (DNA, FACE-1, clone-host, MEANING, constellation) may obscure meaning for new readers.
No mention of automated tests or CI checks for the responsive fixes.
notes: A verified project log describing four DNA-rendered overlay surfaces and a responsive-layout sweep, with a warning that prior channel-view verification was false and Tim has not yet validated on his device.
questions: What exactly is DNA in this codebase and where is it defined?
What are the concrete adapter and archetype interfaces for each surface?
What does FACE-1 mean as a naming convention?
What is the SurfaceNav / orphan-screens fix referenced as the only reachability path?
What is the honest-state discipline item (board://item-c37e7864) and how does it govern verification?
What is the friction record that documents the access-path gap?
What does 'MEANING' refer to as the search mechanism for the transcript corpus?
What specific devices and viewport sizes are required for final verification?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document the FACE-1 breadth surfaces and the phone-frame FORM sweep; the content matches that purpose and adds verification claims, commits, and open gaps.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a proven, repeatable way to build operator-facing overlay surfaces by cloning DNA organisms rather than custom rendering, and to enforce honest, viewport-level verification of responsive layout.
novelty: The clone-host pattern applied uniformly across four distinct surfaces, routing live data through DNA.faceRecord adapters and DNA.renderArchetype archetypes, plus an explicit phone-frame FORM sweep that revealed falsely-verified responsive layouts.
contribution: A self-contained record of a reusable surface-building pattern, the specific organisms, adapters, routes, and commits involved, the verification caveats, and an unresolved operator-device verification gap that a fused system would keep as context.
summary_for_embedding: A living noticeboard guide records the FACE-1 breadth surfaces (channel, board, session drill, transcript visualizer) built with a reusable DNA clone-host pattern and a phone-frame responsive-form sweep that fixed three surfaces after false verification claims.
intention_for_embedding: To document and verify the architecture, implementation commits, and remaining verification gaps of the Company's operator-facing breadth surfaces.
